venues.name: Grass Rootes
venues.description: Farm-to-table in a historic tobacco warehouse turned Civil War jail. Wood-burning fireplace, live music, sophisticated food with casual energy.
venues.when_it_shines: Weekend dinners with live music, cozy winter nights by the fire, date nights that feel special without being stuffy
venues.founder_notes: This is where Culpeper shows off. Historic building with soul, the kind of place that makes you forget you're in a small town. The fireplace in winter hits different.

venues.name: Beer Hound Brewery
venues.description: First brewery in historic downtown. Karaoke, trivia, country line dancing, live music Saturdays. Dog-friendly patio. Local pub energy.
venues.when_it_shines: Saturday night live music, trivia Fridays, sunny afternoons on the patio with your dog, post-work beer with the crew
venues.founder_notes: The heartbeat of downtown. Karaoke nights get wild. This is where locals actually hang, not just tourists passing through.

venues.name: Raven's Nest Coffee House
venues.description: The community living room. Exposed brick, pressed tin ceiling, lush plants, comfy couches. Serves coffee, craft beer, and wine. Hipsters meet middle-aged folk.
venues.when_it_shines: Morning coffee before the world wakes up, afternoon work sessions with natural light, quiet evenings with a book and wine
venues.founder_notes: This is Culpeper's third place. Not home, not work - the spot where community actually happens. Giant scones, friendliest staff, zero pretension.

venues.name: Far Gohn Brewing Company
venues.description: Tavern brewery celebrating German immigrant beer tradition. Classic German ales: Kolsch, Hefeweizen, Altbier. Pizza and nachos. Small, pre-prohibition tavern vibe.
venues.when_it_shines: Saturday afternoons, laid-back weeknight beers, when you want quality brews without the scene
venues.founder_notes: For beer nerds who appreciate craft without the craft beer attitude. Small, intimate, authentic German styles you won't find elsewhere in Culpeper.

venues.name: Frost Cafe
venues.description: Classic 50s diner with booth jukeboxes. Breakfast bar, high ceilings, retro charm. Feels like grandma's kitchen. Traditional diner basics done right.
venues.when_it_shines: Saturday morning breakfast with family, nostalgic solo breakfast at the counter, comfort food hangovers
venues.founder_notes: Pure nostalgia. This is the main street cafe that should exist in every small town but mostly doesn't anymore. Cash-only vibes, actual community regulars.

venues.name: Mamma's
venues.description: Overlooked gem serving Italian comfort food. The kind of place locals know about but tourists miss. Solid, unpretentious, reliable.
venues.when_it_shines: Weeknight family dinners, when you want Italian without a scene, regulars-only vibe
venues.founder_notes: This is exactly what LoKey is for - the spot that gets overlooked on Google but has been feeding families for years. No Instagram energy, just good food.

venues.name: Dtom Dtun
venues.description: Hidden gem sushi spot with huge portions at reasonable prices. Cute, unassuming, unexpectedly excellent.
venues.when_it_shines: Date nights on a budget, when you're craving sushi in a town that shouldn't have good sushi, low-key celebrations
venues.founder_notes: Proof that Culpeper has depth. You wouldn't expect great sushi here, which is exactly why it matters. Another overlooked gem doing it quietly.

venues.name: Albana's Taverna
venues.description: Authentic Greek taverna. House-made, fresh, a little corner of Greece in Culpeper. Family-owned energy, cultural authenticity.
venues.when_it_shines: When you want to travel without leaving town, exploratory food dates, supporting immigrant-owned businesses
venues.founder_notes: Cultural richness over star ratings - exactly the LoKey ethos. This is soul food in the truest sense. Family recipes, real hospitality, zero corporate polish.
